para_trans: Human wildlife in the state includes the conflict monkey (Rhesus macaque (Semnopithecus) / apes entellus).
On ensuring mechanisms for rescue capture and release into habitat.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Decision No: WLP 11.25/प R.No.306 / F-1
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032.
Date: April 22, 2026.
Reference: - 1) Revenue and Forest Department, Government Decision No. WLP: 11.25/प R. No. 306 / F-1,
2) Ma. The minutes of the meeting held under the chairmanship of the Minister (Forests) dated 09.12.2025.

Preamble: - Reference No. 1 for rescue capture and release of human wildlife conflict monkeys (Rhesus macaque) / apes (Semnopithecus entellus) in the state. 1. to ensure the functioning of the Government in accordance with its decisions.
It has come. However, to improve the economic parameters prescribed for capturing conflict monkeys / apes.

The request is being made repeatedly by the people's representatives. Also, Reference No. 2 To revise the economic parameters prescribed for capture of conflict monkeys / apes as per the minutes of the above meeting.
The matter was under the consideration of the government.
The Government's Decisions:
Reference No. The rates fixed for the purpose of capturing monkeys / apes mentioned in section 1 are hereby revised to cover all cases where monkeys / apes are safely caught and released for rescue.
The rate should be paid at Rs.600 per monkey. These rates will be applicable from the date of the Government decision.
Reference No. The other provisions in section 1 shall remain in force.
The government decision is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra, www.maharashtra.gov, in the code 202604221619226019. The order was digitally signed and authenticated.
It's coming.
By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra,
(Rising clouds)
Officer on Special Duty
Revenue and Forest Department
For the: -
1) Private Secretary to Hon'ble Minister (Forests) 2) Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Chief of Forest Force), Maharashtra State, Nagpur
3) Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Wildlife), Maharashtra State, Nagpur
4) All Additional Principal Chief Conservators of Forests 5) All members of the State Wildlife Advisory Board
6) The respective Honorary Wildlife Guard
7) All Chief Conservators of Forests (Wildlife and Territorial)
8) F-1 Executive, Revenue and Forest Department (Selection)
